response: The internet originated in the 1960s with U.S. Defense Department-funded research, leading to ARPANET in 1969 and the adoption of TCP/IP protocols in 1983. Key milestones include Tim Berners-Lee’s invention of the World Wide Web in 1989, the 1993 launch of the Mosaic browser, and the dot-com boom (and subsequent crash) in the late 1990s, which saw survivors like Amazon and Google dominate. The 2000s brought social media (Facebook, YouTube) and mobile internet (iPhone, 2007), while cloud computing (AWS, 2006) revolutionized data storage. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people, shaping global commerce, communication, and daily life.
